intro: Hieracium scotocephalum, also known as Scottish hawkweed, is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, inflammation, and infections, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium scotocephalum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Hieracium scotocephalum is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory infections.
